title: Zoro Stands Against Cabaji
description: On pages 13–16, Zoro faces Cabaji while Buggy’s crew tries to exploit his injuries and turn the duel into a spectacle.
scene: Zoro holds his ground against Cabaji’s tricks, treating the fight as a matter of pride rather than a simple brawl.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: YOU'RE TIRED? HEH... WHAT'S WRONG? ANEMIC?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you've finally given up! Well, I'm not surprised...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's amazing you can still stand in your condi-tion.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You've got it wrong. What I'm tired of...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Very well! No more tricks! I'll finish you now...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: GGH...! HOW COULD THESE COMMON THIEVES HAVE BEATEN US? WE'RE THE BUGGY PIRATE GANG-THE SCOURGE OF THE SEAS!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: HOW COULD THINGS HAVE GONE THIS FAR...?

summary: The part reinforces Zoro’s discipline and pride, showing that the Straw Hats will not let Buggy’s crew define the terms of the fight.
